LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF SAN FRANCISCO FEDERAL CREDIT UNION'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS. THE COURT CONFIRMS THE TENTATIVE RULING. DENIED. A GENERAL DENIAL IS SUFFICIENT TO PUT IN ISSUE ALL THE MATERIAL ALLEGATIONS OF THE COMPLAINT (CCP 431.30(D)) AND THUS NO MORE IS NEEDED TO WITHSTAND A MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS. EVEN THOUGH IT IS NOT OPPOSED, BECAUSE THE MOTION LACKS MERIT, IT IS DENIED. THE PROPER MOTION, IF THERE ARE TRULY NO DISPUTED MATERIAL FACTS, IS A SUMMARY JUDGMENT MOTION. THE PREVAILING PARTY SHALL SUBMIT A [PROPOSED] WRITTEN ORDER THAT TRACKS THE COURT'S RULING, VERBATIM AND A JUDGMENT ACCORDINGLY. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN: J. RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: REYES F. HUNTER, CSR NO. 6576, E-MAIL: RHUNTER@SFTC.ORG.

MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS
Off Calendar Nov-08-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

PLAINTIFF SAN FRANCISCO FEDERAL CREDIT UNION'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS is DENIED. A general denial is sufficient to put in issue all the material allegations of the complaint (CCP 431.30(d)) and thus no more is needed to withstand a motion for judgment on the pleadings. Even though it is not opposed, because the motion lacks merit, it is denied. The proper motion, if there are truly no disputed material facts, is a summary judgment motion. The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim and a judgment accordingly.

Judge: Harold Kahn: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Reyes F. Hunter, CSR No. 6576, e-mail: rhunter@sftc.org. (L&M302)

Later, after calendar, attorney Melanie D. Poppoer of the law offices of Revelatory Center for Arts and The Law (800-678-8476) appears and obtains from the clerk the ruling and instructions for submitting proposed orders.
